lines.en_lit: because he must recite havdala in Who graciously grants knowledge, It is tanna’im, as it was taught Anyone who requires immersion immerses in his manner on Yom Kippur, washing. A menstruating woman and a new mother, immerse in their manner on the night of Yom Kippur.
One who had a seminal emission immerse at any point until the afternoon prayer, Rabbi Yosei says: at any point in the day.

raises a contradiction the zav, and the zava, the male and female leper, and one who had relations with a menstruating woman, and one who is ritually impure a corpse, immerse in their manner on Yom Kippur. A menstruating woman and a woman after childbirth immerse in their manner on the night of Yom Kippur. One who had a seminal emission immerses at any point in the day. Rabbi Yosei says: From the afternoon prayer onward he is not able to immerse.
not difficult. This where he prayed the ne’ila prayer That where he had not prayed
If he had already prayed what is the reasoning of the Rabbis, The Rabbis hold that immersion at its proper time a mitzva,

By inference, Rabbi Yosei holds not a mitzva. But wasn’t it taught If name is written flesh, he not wash and he not smear and he not stand in a filthy place If his time came for an immersion a mitzva, wraps a reed around it and descends and immerses. Rabbi Yosei says: He descends and immerses in his manner provided he does not rub
And we maintain they disagree as to immersion at its time is a mitzva.
This is Rabbi Yosei bar Yehuda, as it was taught Rabbi Yosei bar Yehuda says: It is sufficient for the immersion to be at the end.

The Sages taught: One who sees semen on Yom Kippur descends and immerses. And in the evening, he should rub in the evening? What was, was. Rather, say he should rub from the evening This holds it is a mitzva to rub.
A tanna taught before Rav Naḥman: one who sees semen on Yom Kippur, his sins are forgiven. But wasn’t it taught His sins are arranged What arranged? They are arranged forgiven.

The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught: One who sees semen on Yom Kippur should worry the whole year But if he survives the year, he can be assured a share the World-to-Come. Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: Know as the whole world is hungry and he is satiated When Rav Dimi came from Eretz Yisrael one will live a long life, grow, and raise others.
